commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Because you say in thought Depart from me, ye wicked, who plot against the servants of God, and think to cast them out of the cities of their habitation; as if they had received them in vain, and to no purpose. (Challoner)
Thy cities. Sixtus V, read "their. "(Calmet)
Schismatics, and all innovators, endeavour to withdraw all their dependencies from the truth; (St. Augustine) and infidels wish to prevent the propagation of the gospel. (Berthier)
The enemies of Nehemias, may be here meant, 2 Esdras iv. 2. (Calmet)
Depart from me, all you who would represent the felicity of heaven to be a dream. (Worthington)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
